title: escallonia × bracteata
intro: Escallonia × bracteata, also known as Escallonia, is a genus of flowering plants in the family Escalloniaceae, native to South America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Escallonia include its ability to treat wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues, as well as its potential to reduce fever and alleviate pain. In herbal preparations, Escallonia is often used to create teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be applied topically or ingested to harness its medicinal properties.
